initial_program:
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Nonzero cells
non_zero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Candidate rectangle:
%   every row in the interval must contain a nonzero and every column
%   must contain a nonzero.
rect_candidate(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    R1 =< R2, C1 =< C2,
    not exists R where
        R1 =< R, R =< R2,
        #count{C,V : non_zero(R,C,V), C1 =< C, C =< C2} = 0,
    not exists C where
        C1 =< C, C =< C2,
        #count{R,V : non_zero(R,C,V), R1 =< R, R =< R2} = 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Count of nonzero cells inside a rectangle
rect_count(R1,R2,C1,C2,Nc) :-
    Nc = #count{R,C,V : non_zero(R,C,V), R1 =< R, R =< R2, C1 =< C, C =< C2}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Maximal count among all candidate rectangles
max_nonzero(M) :-
    M = #max{Rc :
        #count{R,C,V : non_zero(R,C,V), R1 =< R, R =< R2, C1 =< C, C =< C2},
        where rect_candidate(R1,R2,C1,C2)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Component rectangle:
%   a rectangle that satisfies the candidate condition and whose nonzero
%   count equals the maximal count.
component_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    rect_candidate(R1,R2,C1,C2),
    rect_count(R1,R2,C1,C2,Count),
    Count = max_nonzero(Max).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the transposed rectangle
trans_h(H) :- H = C2 - C1 + 1.
trans_w(W) :- W = R2 - R1 + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Placement of the transposed rectangle  it must not intersect any
% component cell (any nonzero cell that lies inside the component rectangle).
placement(PRow,PCol) :-
    0 =< PRow, PRow + trans_h - 1 < n_rows(N),
    0 =< PCol, PCol + trans_w - 1 < n_cols(N),
    not exists R,C where
        component_cell(R,C,V),
        R1 =< R, R =< R2,
        C1 =< C, C =< C2,
        PRow =< R, R =< PRow + trans_h - 1,
        PCol =< C, C =< PCol + trans_w - 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% A component cell: a nonzero cell that belongs to the component rectangle.
component_cell(R,C,V) :- non_zero(R,C,V), R1 =< R, R =< R2, C1 =< C, C =< C2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Output for the transposed cells.
output(R,C,Vc) :-
    component_cell(Rc,Cc,Vc),
    R = PRow + (Cc - C1),
    C = PCol + (Rc - R1),
    0 =< R, R < n_rows(N),
    0 =< C, C < n_cols(N).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells that are not part of the transposed region must stay zero.
:- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0,
    not exists Rc,Cc,Vc,R1,R2,C1,C2,PRow,PCol where
        component_cell(Rc,Cc,Vc),
        R = PRow + (Cc - C1),
        C = PCol + (Rc - R1).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour for each cell.
cell(R,C) :- 0 =< R, R < n_rows(N), 0 =< C, C < n_cols(N).
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Nonzero cells
non_zero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Candidate rectangle:
%   every row in the interval must contain a nonzero and every column
%   must contain a nonzero.
rect_candidate(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    R1 =< R2, C1 =< C2,
    not exists R where
        R1 =< R, R =< R2,
        #count{C,V : non_zero(R,C,V), C1 =< C, C =< C2} = 0,
    not exists C where
        C1 =< C, C =< C2,
        #count{R,V : non_zero(R,C,V), R1 =< R, R =< R2} = 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Count of nonzero cells inside a rectangle
rect_count(R1,R2,C1,C2,Nc) :-
    Nc = #count{R,C,V : non_zero(R,C,V), R1 =< R, R =< R2, C1 =< C, C =< C2}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Maximal count among all candidate rectangles
max_nonzero(M) :-
    M = #max{Rc :
        #count{R,C,V : non_zero(R,C,V), R1 =< R, R =< R2, C1 =< C, C =< C2},
        where rect_candidate(R1,R2,C1,C2)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Component rectangle:
%   a rectangle that satisfies the candidate condition and whose nonzero
%   count equals the maximal count.
component_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    rect_candidate(R1,R2,C1,C2),
    rect_count(R1,R2,C1,C2,Count),
    Count = max_nonzero(Max).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the transposed rectangle
trans_h(H) :- H = C2 - C1 + 1.
trans_w(W) :- W = R2 - R1 + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Placement of the transposed rectangle  it must not intersect any
% component cell (any nonzero cell that lies inside the component rectangle).
placement(PRow,PCol) :-
    0 =< PRow, PRow + trans_h - 1 < n_rows(N),
    0 =< PCol, PCol + trans_w - 1 < n_cols(N),
    not exists R,C where
        component_cell(R,C,V),
        R1 =< R, R =< R2,
        C1 =< C, C =< C2,
        PRow =< R, R =< PRow + trans_h - 1,
        PCol =< C, C =< PCol + trans_w - 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% A component cell: a nonzero cell that belongs to the component rectangle.
component_cell(R,C,V) :- non_zero(R,C,V), R1 =< R, R =< R2, C1 =< C, C =< C2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Output for the transposed cells.
output(R,C,Vc) :-
    component_cell(Rc,Cc,Vc),
    R = PRow + (Cc - C1),
    C = PCol + (Rc - R1),
    0 =< R, R < n_rows(N),
    0 =< C, C < n_cols(N).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells that are not part of the transposed region must stay zero.
:- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0,
    not exists Rc,Cc,Vc,R1,R2,C1,C2,PRow,PCol where
        component_cell(Rc,Cc,Vc),
        R = PRow + (Cc - C1),
        C = PCol + (Rc - R1).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour for each cell.
cell(R,C) :- 0 =< R, R < n_rows(N), 0 =< C, C < n_cols(N).
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- cell(R,C).
